figures.description: A plot summarizing crowdworker preferences for a variety of models, including context-distilled models, RLHF models trained on a ‘static’ dataset, and RLHF models trained by an iterated ‘online’ method for either helpfulness and harmlessness (HH) or for helpfulness only.
A summary of the full training process.

key_findings_summary: The paper demonstrates a method to train a helpful and harmless (HH) natural language assistant by collecting human preference data and applying preference modeling (PM) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). The trained models are preferred over a 52B parameter context-distilled model in crowdworker evaluations. The paper highlights a tension between helpfulness and harmlessness during RLHF training and explores techniques like iterated online RLHF and out-of-distribution detection to manage it.
